summary: Tested NotaryChain Enhanced KBA identity-verification flow end-to-end. Backend (8/8 pytest) and Frontend (Dashboard banner, /kba-test card, modal Step 1->Step 2 transition) all pass. Notarization gate confirmed: 403 'identity_verification_required' before verify -> 200/201 after PASS. Frontend selfie/quiz steps not exercised in the browser (camera not available in headless), but covered via backend tests.

minor.issue: Occasional 502 from ingress on cold path (took 17s+ on first call) due to synchronous Hedera HCS topic creation. Resolves on retry. Consider async/lazy HCS provisioning or higher gateway timeout.

design_issues.screen: EnhancedKBAFlow modal + DocumentAnalysisStep + many other pages
issues: React hydration console errors: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>/<select>'. The visual-editor instrumentation injects <span data-ve-dynamic="true"> wrappers around <option>/<select> children. Dev-only warning; does not break functionality, but pollutes console and can hide real issues.

action_items: (Optional) Investigate the React hydration warning from VE instrumentation around <option> children — not introduced by this task but visible across multiple pages.
(Optional) Make Hedera HCS topic creation async or background-queued in POST /api/notary/requests to avoid the 502/long latency on first call.
critical_code_review_comments: answer-quiz weighted scoring (30% doc + 30% face + 40% quiz) is sensible; with real-world AI rejecting blank inputs the flow fails closed by design — good security posture.
/api/auth/me response does not appear to include 'role' (only checked with `if me.get('role') is not None`). Verify it's intentional; some clients may rely on role at /me.
kba_enhanced_sessions document uses dotted updates (document_ocr.confidence) — consistent with the route schema; OK.

EnhancedKBAFlow modal uses native <select> with <option> children but the visual-editor wraps them in <span>, producing React hydration warnings. Consider opting these elements out of VE instrumentation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical mounted-modal + gate-banner + step transitions; selfie/quiz UI not exercised due to headless camera limitation

test_credentials: demo@test.com / Demo123! (client). admin@notarychain.com / Admin123!.  IMPORTANT: demo user was reset to identity_verified=false at end of this run.
seed_data_creation: Test 06 directly seeded high doc_ocr.confidence (90) and face_match.similarity (88) into the in-flight kba_enhanced_sessions doc to exercise the PASS path. This is required because GPT-5.2 Vision correctly rejects synthetic blank PNGs we can supply from tests; production real-photo flows naturally produce high scores.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test /app/backend/tests/test_enhanced_kba_flow.py is end-to-end: (1) resets demo@test.com identity_verified to false via direct Mongo, (2) verifies 403 identity_verification_required, (3) runs the enhanced KBA happy-path, (4) seeds high doc/face confidence to exercise the PASS path (since vision rejects synthetic test images — by design), (5) confirms /auth/me identity_verified=true, (6) confirms POST /api/notary/requests returns 201. The frontend /request-notarization gate (handleSubmit catches 403 -> opens enhanced-kba-modal) was NOT clicked-through end-to-end because the page is a multi-step wizard requiring file upload + identity step before reaching submit; the underlying 403 path is proven by the backend test. Dashboard banner + /kba-test launch + Step1->Step2 transition fully validated via Playwright. demo@test.com was reset to identity_verified=false at the end of this run.
